02-REQ-009.note: Real-time committed-state sync without per-turn polling — dedupe with 04-REQ-053 into one delivery requirement; graduated from the module-02 parked ledger.

02-REQ-010.note: Invisibility filtered at the data layer, keyed on the querying connection's team; the data-layer-enforcement principle half was already covered by global-invariants/security-enforced-outside-the-library — what retires here is the invisibility feature's own visibility semantics. Graduated from the module-02 parked ledger.

02-REQ-041.note: Spectators connect read-only under a spectator access token, filtered as opponents of every team (02-REVIEW-007). Transport binding (WebSocket) is mechanism — the authored behaviour is transport-neutral. Graduates out of the parked ledger's operator-control 'Client connection topology' entry; 02-REQ-038/039/040 remain parked.

03-REQ-031.note: Every admitted connection subject to the invisibility filter; spectators filtered on opponent terms — dedupe with 02-REQ-010 and 04-REQ-047 into the single filtering requirement.

04-REQ-037.note: Turn resolution as a single atomic transaction with no observable intermediate state — dedupe with 04-REQ-056/067 into one atomic-delivery requirement.

04-REQ-047.note: Data-layer filtering of invisible opponent snake state; spectators and opponents filtered equally; board effects stay observable. Dedupe with 02-REQ-010 and 03-REQ-031.

04-REQ-048.note: Filtering applies to historical queries — scrubbing cannot recover an invisible-at-that-turn opponent snake.

04-REQ-049.note: Only the snake's own state record is hidden; items consumed and turn events remain visible to all observers, with no tracking of whether the causer was visible.

04-REQ-050.note: Allied connections observe the invisible snake normally with visibility false exposed so the client can indicate it.

04-REQ-051.note: Visibility-filter transitions happen exactly at turn boundaries, in both directions.

04-REQ-052.note: SPLIT, retired here as the matrix's owning row: the no-client-side-filtering / no-cooperating-client half is authored here; the staged-move read policy (own-team-only) is operator-control's substance; attribution-metadata blocking is identity-and-authorization/admission-records-private.

04-REQ-053.note: Incremental subscription delivery as turns commit, no polling — dedupe with 02-REQ-009.

04-REQ-054.note: The four supported observation patterns: live current-turn view, historical scrubbing, turn-transition animation, full-game mid-join catch-up.

04-REQ-055.note: Subscription deliveries filtered on the same terms as direct queries — folded into the filtering requirement's 'deliveries and query results' wording.

04-REQ-056.note: A turn's commit delivered as a single logical update — dedupe with 04-REQ-037/067.

04-REQ-057.note: Any completed turn reconstructible from the query surface within authorized visibility: snakes, items, budgets, full event list.

04-REQ-058.note: Reconstruction requires no re-execution of game-rule logic; per-turn state directly readable as committed.

04-REQ-067.note: No pre-commit delivery to subscribers — dedupe with 04-REQ-037/056.

04-REQ-071.note: Per-turn scoreboard rows for every rostered team, zero-filled, computed over the true alive set including invisible snakes, identical for all connections, the sole aggregate channel. Same-transaction write constraint-mined into #never-lags-the-snapshot.

05-REQ-067.note: Retired here as the matrix's owning row, authored abstractly as 'team-private live state' (the legacy enumeration of bot-side state kinds is outside this capability's dependency ceiling). Coach designation/storage half is team-management's (05-REQ-068a); coach admission terms are identity-and-authorization/coach-tokens; read-only standing is identity-and-authorization/role-bound-privileges.

05-REQ-068b.note: Coach access confers nothing for finished games beyond what every authenticated user has — the role is meaningful only at the live boundary.

06-REQ-032.note: SPLIT, retired here as the matrix's owning row: the live read-scoping substance (team members, coaches, admins only; never cross-team) is authored here; the finished-game/replay read half is replay-and-audit's; the team-configuration access half is bot-configuration's. Cross-team denial per 06-REVIEW-002.

08-REQ-027l.note: Room lobby links to the spectating view of its playing game.

08-REQ-038.note: Invisibility rendered own-team-only, never revealing other teams' invisibility states. The effect-outline styling (blue/red outlines, shimmer treatment) is presentation mechanism and stays in code.

08-REQ-052a.note: Coach mode renders the full live interface read-only, reachable from where the live game is surfaced (including the spectating view).

08-REQ-052b.note: Coach mode exposes no affordance that writes on behalf of the coached team — controls hidden or visibly disabled.

08-REQ-052c.note: Coach inspection is client-local: no writes, no selection shadow, no displacement, at most one inspected snake per coach client, coexisting with operators' real selection shadows.

08-REQ-052d.note: Coach inspection visually and gesturally distinct from operator selection.

08-REQ-080.note: Spectating available for any playing game to every authenticated user, without team membership.

08-REQ-081.note: Entry obtains a spectator access token via the platform's issuance path and connects under it (identity-and-authorization/spectator-tokens owns issuance).

08-REQ-082.note: The spectating view subscribes to the live current-state pattern and renders board, items, observable snakes, and events in real time.

08-REQ-083.note: The UI honours the server-side filter and never infers or reconstructs invisible-snake state from any channel.

08-REQ-084.note: Scoreboard sourced exclusively from the server aggregate channel, rendered exactly as delivered — dedupe with 08-REQ-084b and 04-REQ-071 (08-REVIEW-018 posture).

08-REQ-084b.note: Never compute team-level aggregates client-side from per-snake data — folded into the sole-aggregate-authority requirement.

08-REQ-085.note: Spectating displays current turn number and, per team, remaining budget and declared-turn-over status.

08-REQ-086.note: Spectating exposes zero mutating affordances — structural read-only, matching the token's terms.

08-REQ-087.note: Timeline scrubber over all completed turns; up-front full-history subscription on entry per 08-REVIEW-019; scrubbing never interrupts the live subscription.

08-REQ-088.note: Scrubbed view visibly not-live with a one-action return to the live head.

08-REQ-089.note: Subscription released and spectator token discarded on navigation away or game finish.

02-REVIEW-007.note: Spectator intersection semantics: spectators see no invisible snake of any team — treated as opponents of every team.

03-REVIEW-009.note: Spectator eligibility policy (private games, visibility, rate limits) deliberately deferred; the scenario records that authentication + game-in-play are the only gates until the requirement is deliberately revised.

04-REVIEW-009.note: No subscription delivery-order guarantee: a turn's events are a set; deterministic order is read from the stored canonical representation order.

04-REVIEW-012.note: Turn-0 starting positions and initial food are fully public; the filter encodes invisibility only, never pre-game positional privacy.

04-REVIEW-020.note: Scoreboard computed over the true alive set including invisible snakes; eliminated teams zero-filled, never omitted.

04-REVIEW-021.note: Live team score is the normalised as-if-ended score (par 1.0) per game-engine/scoring, decoupled from aggregate length.

06-REVIEW-002.note: Strict team-scoping of team-private live state; cross-team reads denied; admins read as implicit coaches, designated coaches on member terms.

08-REVIEW-018.note: Clients are dumb readers: server-side aggregate view is the sole scoreboard source; client-side aggregation of per-snake data is prohibited (would leak/under-count under invisibility).

08-REVIEW-019.note: Up-front full-history subscription on spectating entry; bounded entry latency accepted; no lazy-fetch state machine.
